meta.purpose: Compliance reference data for ScopeLock professional brief generation. Every scope statement generated by the AI must be grounded in at least one entry from this file.

NCC_Volume_Two_2022.full_title: National Construction Code 2022 — Volume Two: Housing Provisions
NCC_Volume_Two_2022.applies_to: Class 1 buildings (detached houses, semi-detached, townhouses) and Class 10 associated structures

Part_H1_Structure.description: Structural reliability — all structural elements must satisfy NCC H1 performance requirements. Framing, footings, slabs, lintels and bracing to comply with AS 1684 (timber framing), AS 3600 (concrete), AS 4600 (cold-formed steel), or verified by an engineer to AS 1170.

Part_H2_Damp_and_Weatherproofing.description: External walls, roofs, and subfloor must resist the penetration of water. Sarking, flashings, and cavity construction to comply. Subfloor ventilation minimum 3500mm² per linear metre of external wall.

Part_H3_Fire_Safety.description: Smoke alarms mandatory in every bedroom, every storey, and interconnected throughout. Garage-to-habitable separation: 90/90/90 fire-rated wall or equivalent — no direct door opening permitted. BAL (Bushfire Attack Level) assessment required in designated bushfire-prone areas.

Part_H4_Health_and_Amenity.description: Habitable rooms require a minimum ceiling height of 2400mm (2700mm preferred). Natural light minimum 10% of floor area. Natural ventilation minimum 5% of floor area (or mechanical equivalent). Sanitary facilities: minimum one toilet, hand basin, and shower or bath per dwelling.

Part_H5_Sound_Insulation.description: For Class 1a buildings sharing a wall with another dwelling: Rw+Ctr ≥ 50 for walls, Rw ≥ 50 for floors. Not applicable to fully detached dwellings but best practice is to specify acoustic insulation to party walls.

Part_H6_Energy_Efficiency.reference: NCC 2022 Vol.2 Part H6 (formerly Section J / Part 3.12)
Part_H6_Energy_Efficiency.description: New homes must achieve minimum 7-star NatHERS (Nationwide House Energy Rating Scheme) energy rating, or comply via DTS (Deemed-to-Satisfy) pathway. Ceiling insulation minimum R3.5 for most climate zones. Wall insulation minimum R1.5. High-performance double-glazed windows (U-value ≤ 2.0, SHGC per climate zone). Solar access and orientation must be considered in design.

Part_H7_Livable_Housing.reference: NCC 2022 Vol.2 Part H7 — Livable Housing Design
Part_H7_Livable_Housing.description: Mandatory for new Class 1a dwellings from May 2023: minimum Silver level Livable Housing Australia (LHA) standard. Requires step-free path from street to a level entry point, step-free internal access to at least a toilet and shower, wider corridor clearances (900mm clear), reinforced bathroom walls for future grab rail installation.

AS_3740_2010.full_title: AS 3740-2010 Waterproofing of Domestic Wet Areas
AS_3740_2010.applies_to: All domestic wet areas including showers, baths, bathrooms, ensuites, laundries, and wet rooms in Class 1 and Class 10 buildings

water_exposure_classifications.Type_1: Shower recess floor and 150mm up walls — minimum 0.9mm membrane
water_exposure_classifications.Type_2: Shower walls to 1800mm height — continuous membrane
water_exposure_classifications.Type_3: Entire bathroom floor and 100mm up walls — applies where floor drain is present outside shower
water_exposure_classifications.Type_4: Full floor and wall waterproofing — applies to wet rooms, walk-in showers, and steam rooms

critical_requirements.shower_floor: Minimum R10 slip resistance (AS 4586), fall to drain minimum 1:60, membrane to 150mm up all abutting walls
critical_requirements.shower_walls: Membrane to full height of shower walls minimum 1800mm AFF, all penetrations sealed
critical_requirements.bath_surrounds: Membrane 75mm above bath rim to all adjacent walls and floor under bath
critical_requirements.laundry: Full floor waterproofing where floor waste is installed, upstand minimum 75mm to all walls
critical_requirements.membrane_types: Sheet membrane, liquid-applied membrane, or ceramic tile with flexible grout — all to manufacturers specification and AS 3740 requirements
critical_requirements.inspection: Waterproofing membrane must be inspected and approved by building surveyor prior to tiling
critical_requirements.subfloor_drainage: AS 3740 compliant fall to drain in shower recess: 1:60 minimum gradient

AS_3740_2010.compliance_note: Non-compliant waterproofing is the single most common cause of building defect litigation in Australia. Always specify inspection hold point.

AS_4586_2013.full_title: AS 4586-2013 Slip Resistance Classification of New Pedestrian Surface Materials
AS_4586_2013.applies_to: All pedestrian floor surfaces in wet and dry areas

classifications.P3: Wet areas with controlled foot traffic — bathrooms, ensuites, powder rooms (minimum for residential wet areas)
classifications.P4: Wet areas with uncontrolled barefoot traffic — showers, pool surrounds, change rooms (minimum for shower floors)
classifications.P5: Steep slopes or extreme wet exposure — external ramps, pool steps
classifications.R10: General external paving, driveways, paths — minimum slip resistance for outdoor pedestrian surfaces
classifications.R11: Commercial kitchens, utility areas, heavily trafficked external areas
classifications.A: Dry internal areas — minimum for residential floor finishes in dry areas
classifications.B: Dry areas with moderate traffic — corridors, living areas
classifications.C: High-traffic dry areas and transition zones

residential_minimums.shower_floor: P4 minimum, P5 recommended for textured tiles
residential_minimums.external_paving: R10 minimum, R11 for pool surrounds and external ramps
residential_minimums.internal_transition_zones: P3 where wet-to-dry transition occurs (e.g., bathroom threshold)

AS_4586_2013.testing_method: Ramp test (German DIN 51130) for R-ratings, wet pendulum test for P-ratings. Supplier must provide slip resistance certificate.

Part_1_Water_Services.key_requirements: Cold water service pressure minimum 100 kPa at all fixtures. Hot water system must comply with local authority requirements. Backflow prevention device required on all hose taps and irrigation systems. Pipe material: copper (AS 1432), CPVC, or PE-X to AS 2492.

Part_2_Sanitary_Plumbing.key_requirements: Minimum drain grades: 50mm pipe at 1:40, 100mm pipe at 1:60. All drains to be accessible or flushing point provided. Every fixture requires a trap (minimum 75mm seal). Vent pipes required for all sanitary fixtures.

Part_3_Stormwater.key_requirements: Roof drainage must discharge to legal point of discharge or approved retention system. On-site detention (OSD) may be required by council. Downpipes: 50mm minimum diameter, one per 30m² roof area. Overflow provisions required for all roof areas.

Part_4_Heated_Water.key_requirements: Hot water systems must maintain storage temperature ≥ 60°C and deliver at risk outlets ≤ 50°C (residential). Tempering valve mandatory on all shower and bath outlets in residential dwellings. Solar hot water systems to AS/NZS 2712. Heat pump water heaters to AS/NZS 4234.

AS_NZS_3500_2021.licensed_plumber_required: All plumbing and drainage work must be performed by a licensed plumber and notified to the relevant state/territory authority. A Certificate of Compliance must be issued on completion.

AS_1428_2021.full_title: AS 1428-2021 Design for Access and Mobility

key_requirements.doorways: Minimum 820mm clear opening width for accessible doors
key_requirements.corridors: Minimum 1000mm clear width for accessible paths, 1200mm preferred
key_requirements.floor_surface: Maximum slope 1:14 for accessible paths, non-slip surface required
key_requirements.grab_rails: Reinforcing to bathroom walls to support future grab rail installation (minimum 33kg pull load)

Part_2_Enhanced.key_requirements: Enhanced access provisions for residential buildings targeting full wheelchair accessibility — includes roll-under vanities, adjustable height fixtures, ceiling hoist provisions.

AS_1428_2021.ncc_linkage: NCC 2022 Part H7 Livable Housing Design mandates AS 1428.1 compliance for Silver LHA level.

AS_1288_2006.full_title: AS 1288-2006 Glass in Buildings — Selection and Installation

key_requirements.human_impact_zones: Safety glass (toughened or laminated per AS/NZS 2208) required within 500mm of floor level, in shower screens, in doors, and in side panels adjacent to doors
key_requirements.shower_screens: Minimum 6mm toughened glass to AS/NZS 2208, or safety glass alternative
key_requirements.frameless_glazing: All frameless glass panels in wet areas must be toughened (AS/NZS 2208 Category A or B)
key_requirements.window_falls: Openable windows with sill below 1700mm AFF over a floor height drop ≥ 2m must include falls protection (fixed bar or restricted opening ≤ 125mm)
key_requirements.energy_performance: U-value and SHGC to comply with NCC 2022 Part H6 energy efficiency requirements for the relevant climate zone

AS_4970_2009.full_title: AS 4970-2009 Protection of Trees on Development Sites
AS_4970_2009.applies_to: Any development site where existing trees are to be retained

key_requirements.tpz: Tree Protection Zone (TPZ) = 12 × trunk diameter at breast height (1.4m AFF). No excavation, fill, soil compaction, or chemical storage within TPZ
key_requirements.sre: Structural Root Zone (SRZ) = inner 25% of TPZ radius. Absolutely no excavation or root severance within SRZ
key_requirements.protection_fencing: Solid barrier fencing (not shade cloth) at TPZ boundary before any site works commence — must remain in place until all hard landscaping complete
key_requirements.arborist_report: Level 5 AQF Arborist report required before any works within or adjacent to TPZ. Report must assess tree health and prescribe protection methodology
key_requirements.council_permit: Most councils require a permit to remove or prune significant trees. Tree removal without permit may result in significant fines.

parts.Part_0: General principles — limit state design, load combinations
parts.Part_1: Permanent, imposed and other actions — dead loads, live loads (floor: 1.5 kPa residential, 3.0 kPa commercial), balcony balustrades 0.75 kN/m horizontal
parts.Part_2: Wind actions — wind region classification (A, B, C, D) determines design wind speed. Cyclonic regions (C, D) require specific structural detailing
parts.Part_3: Snow and ice actions — applies to alpine areas
parts.Part_4: Earthquake actions — required for all new structures in Australia

AS_1170_2002.residential_note: For conventional housing within NCC deemed-to-satisfy scope, AS 1684 (timber framing) provides prescriptive compliance without requiring individual engineering certification.

AS_2890_2004.full_title: AS 2890.1-2004 Parking Facilities — Off-Street Car Parking
AS_2890_2004.applies_to: Garages and carports in residential developments

minimum_dimensions.single_garage: 3000mm wide × 5400mm long (clear internal, door closed) — 3200mm wide preferred
minimum_dimensions.ceiling_height: Minimum 2100mm clear to structure at lowest point
minimum_dimensions.driveway_width: 3000mm minimum single, 5000mm minimum double at front boundary

AS_2890_2004.ncc_linkage: NCC 2022 Part H3 requires 90/90/90 fire-rated separation between garage and habitable areas. No direct door from garage to sleeping areas.

zone_compliance_matrix.description: Maps renovation/construction zones to their mandatory applicable standards. Every scope statement for a zone must address each required standard.

required_standards: AS 3740-2010 (full floor waterproofing where floor waste installed)

mandatory_inclusions: GFCI protection to all bench-top power outlets
Adequate natural light or artificial lighting per NCC H4

required_standards: NCC H6 (EV charging provisions if applicable)
mandatory_inclusions: Self-closing fire door if direct internal access provided
Engineer certification for any wall removal or new openings
Inspection by registered building surveyor at mandatory hold points
Balustrade minimum 1000mm AFF where floor ≥ 1000mm above ground
Structural connection to existing building via engineer-designed detail
Treated timber or hardwood specified to H3 hazard class minimum
Reinforced bathroom walls for grab rails

compliance_language_templates.description: Standard compliance clauses to be inserted verbatim (or paraphrased) in professional briefs.
compliance_language_templates.waterproofing: All wet areas to be waterproofed in full compliance with AS 3740-2010 prior to installation of floor and wall finishes. A mandatory inspection hold point shall be established for the building surveyor to inspect and approve all waterproofing membrane installations before any tiling commences.
compliance_language_templates.slip_resistance: All floor tiles and surface materials within wet areas to achieve minimum P3 classification (bathrooms) or P4 classification (shower recesses) per AS 4586-2013. Supplier slip resistance certificate to be provided prior to installation.
compliance_language_templates.plumbing: All plumbing and drainage works to be performed by a licensed plumber in accordance with AS/NZS 3500 Parts 1–4 and applicable state plumbing regulations. A Certificate of Compliance to be issued on completion of all plumbing works.
compliance_language_templates.glazing: All glass within 500mm of floor level, in shower enclosures, and in all door panels to be safety glass compliant with AS/NZS 2208 and selected per AS 1288-2006.
compliance_language_templates.tempering_valve: Thermostatic mixing valves (tempering valves) to be installed to all shower and bath outlets in accordance with AS/NZS 3500.4, delivering water at a maximum 50°C at the point of use.
compliance_language_templates.fire_separation: Fire separation between attached garage and habitable areas to comply with NCC 2022 Vol.2 Part H3: 90/90/90 fire-rated construction to all shared walls and ceilings, with a self-closing smoke-seal door where direct internal access is provided.
compliance_language_templates.energy_rating: New works to be designed and documented to achieve a minimum 7-star NatHERS energy rating under the NCC 2022 Volume Two Part H6 energy efficiency provisions. Insulation, glazing, and building sealing specifications to be confirmed by an accredited energy assessor.
compliance_language_templates.smoke_alarms: Interconnected photoelectric smoke alarms to be installed in all bedrooms, hallways, and on each storey in accordance with NCC 2022 Vol.2 Part H3 and the relevant state smoke alarm legislation.
compliance_language_templates.structural: All structural elements — including any wall removals, new openings, lintels, and connection details — to be designed and certified by a Registered Professional Engineer (RPEQ/CPEng) and constructed in compliance with NCC 2022 Vol.2 Part H1 and applicable structural standards.
compliance_language_templates.livable_housing: The works are to incorporate Livable Housing Australia (LHA) Silver level design principles per NCC 2022 Vol.2 Part H7, including step-free internal access, minimum 820mm clear doorway openings, and reinforced bathroom walls for future grab rail installation.
